Ad Text:
We are seeking a highly skilled AI Engineer to design, develop, deploy, and maintain AI-driven solutions that deliver measurable business value. This role bridges data science, machine learning, and production engineering, transforming models and prototypes into scalable, secure, and reliable production systems.
 
The AI Engineer will work closely with data scientists, data engineers, product teams, and business stakeholders to implement machine learning and generative AI solutions across the organisation.
 
Key Responsibilities
 
Model Development & Implementation

Design and develop machine learning and AI models for structured and unstructured data.
Implement and optimise models for performance, scalability, and reliability.
Build and fine-tune large language models (LLMs) and generative AI systems where applicable.
Conduct model evaluation, validation, and monitoring.
Deploy models into production using CI/CD pipelines.
Build and maintain model monitoring, retraining, and versioning processes.
Implement automated testing and governance frameworks.
Ensure reliability, performance, and observability of AI systems.
 
Data Engineering & Integration

Work with data engineers to build robust data pipelines.
Integrate AI solutions into enterprise applications and APIs.
Ensure data quality, lineage, and compliance standards are met.
 
Governance, Security & Compliance

Implement responsible AI principles (bias mitigation, explainability, transparency).
Ensure AI solutions comply with privacy, security, and regulatory requirements.
Document model logic, assumptions, and limitations.
 
Collaboration & Strategy

Partner with business stakeholders to identify high-value AI use cases.
Translate business problems into technical AI solutions.
Contribute to AI strategy, architecture, and technology selection.
Mentor junior engineers and promote AI best practices.
 
Key Selection Criteria

Strong programming skills in Python (required); familiarity with R or other languages is advantageous.
Experience with ML frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Scikit-learn.
Experience with LLM frameworks (e.g., LangChain, OpenAI APIs, Hugging Face).
Knowledge of MLOps tools (MLflow, Kubeflow, Azure ML, SageMaker, etc.).
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Familiarity with containerisation (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes).
Experience building APIs (FastAPI, Flask, etc.).
Strong understanding of data structures, algorithms, and software engineering principles.
 
Machine Learning Expertise

Supervised and unsupervised learning techniques.
Feature engineering and model optimisation.
Model validation, evaluation metrics, and monitoring.
Experience with NLP, computer vision, or time-series modelling (preferred).
 
Soft Skills

Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
Ability to communicate complex AI concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Collaborative mindset with cross-functional teams.
Strong documentation and governance discipline.
Consultative manner and approach
 
Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or related field.
Master's or PhD in AI/ML (preferred but not mandatory).
Experience in machine learning or AI engineering roles (adjustable by level).
 
Nice to Have

Experience with generative AI use cases in enterprise environments.
Experience with vector databases
Knowledge of Responsible AI frameworks.
Experience in regulated industries (banking, finance, healthcare, mining, industrial).
Experience working in agile product environments.

Are you an ambitious Senior Engineer ready to step up, or a seasoned Principal looking for a fresh challenge?

This is a rare opportunity to take a high-impact technical leadership role within our growing Automation team. We are looking for a technical powerhouse who thrives on intricate details and possesses a natural instinct for both complex problem-solving and people leadership.

The Opportunity

Joining our client’s progressive team, you will have the autonomy to steer technical strategy and develop team capability on a national stage. You will balance "big picture" roadmap planning with the unique opportunity to remain hands-on, solving complex industrial control challenges. Your influence will be pivotal as you mentor and shape the professional growth of our engineers and technicians, ensuring collective success across the operation.

The Role

Reporting to the Engineering Manager, you will lead and empower a high-performing team of approximately 10 professionals, ensuring technical excellence across the full project lifecycle. Leveraging your mastery, you will oversee a complex portfolio within the energy, infrastructure, mining, and renewables sectors. This is more than a management position; it is a chance to define a new era for the business, giving you the platform to influence strategy and leave a lasting legacy.

Why Newcastle?

The Newcastle region truly offers the best of both worlds: coastal and rural lifestyles, a lower cost of living, and a thriving hub for engineering innovation. 
While we believe in the benefits of this lifestyle, we are open to flexible location arrangements for the right candidate, provided you are committed to regular team engagement and onsite collaboration.

The Business

Join a proud, Australian-owned company and a respected leader in the field. With a flat reporting structure and minimal "red tape," they prioritise action and efficiency over bureaucracy. 

This is a business built on technical expertise and dependable results, powered by a collaborative team of great people who truly thrive together.

Your Expertise

You are a degree-qualified engineer, ideally with 10+ years of experience in heavy manufacturing, mining, or power industries. You bring a proven track record in design, programming, commissioning, and installation of sophisticated automation systems.

Technically, you are highly proficient across major platforms, including Allen-Bradley, Rockwell Automation, Citect SCADA, HMI, and complex industrial control systems.

What’s next?

Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world.

We are seeking an experienced Big Data Engineer to lead the development of scalable, high-performance backend services, with a focus on building and optimizing both traditional search and AI-powered search capabilities. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop robust search systems that support core business use cases.

This role requires strong backend expertise and a commitment to delivering reliable, efficient solutions that drive Binance’s operational excellence.

Design, build, and optimize scalable backend services supporting both traditional search and AI-powered search systems.
Own the architecture of search-related systems to ensure high reliability, performance, and scalability.
Develop reusable backend frameworks and infrastructure to accelerate search and AI product development.
Collaborate closely with data scientists and AI engineers to enable LLM-powered search, ranking, and agent capabilities.
Continuously improve search performance, including indexing, retrieval, ranking, and latency optimization.
Translate business needs into scalable search solutions.
Drive execution and deliver results in complex or evolving environments.
6+ years of backend engineering experience.
Strong proficiency in Java and Python.
Experience designing and building large-scale distributed systems.
Hands-on experience with search technologies (e.g., Elasticsearch, vector search, hybrid retrieval) is preferred.
Experience with big data processing frameworks such as Flink or Spark for real-time or batch data processing.
Familiarity with AI-powered search technologies, including LLM-based search, RAG architectures, or agent frameworks (e.g., Dify, LangChain, Mastra, OpenAI Agents SDK).
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure and scalable system design.
Ability to deliver results in environments with ambiguity or evolving requirements.

Why Binance

• Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem

• Collaborate with world-class talent in a user-centric global organization with a flat structure

• Tackle unique, fast-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment

• Thrive in a results-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning

• Competitive salary and company benefits

• Work-from-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team)

Binance is committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe that having a diverse workforce is fundamental to our success.

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing

Design and implement data pipelines to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from ERP (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, Technology One), HRIS (e.g., Workday, UKG), payroll systems (e.g., Employment Hero, MicrOpay, Payroll Metrics), and enterprise automation platforms.
Develop and maintain data integrations with external financial systems including banking platforms (e.g., payment gateways, direct debit services, reconciliation tools) and taxation systems (e.g., ATO, GST reporting, Single Touch Payroll, PAYG).
Build and manage data flows from recruitment platforms such as LinkedIn, SEEK, and other talent acquisition systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver actionable insights through dashboards, reports, and analytics.
Ensure data quality, integrity, and compliance across all integrated systems.
Monitor and optimize data workflows for performance and reliability.
Maintain comprehensive documentation for data architecture, mappings, and technical specifications.
Work closely with cloud and architecture teams to deploy and manage data solutions in Databricks and Power BI.

 

 

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program 

 

What Sets You Apart

Strong experience in data engineering and analytics, including ETL processes and data modelling.
Proven track record working with ERP, HR, payroll, finance, and recruitment data.
Familiarity with banking data structures, payment processing, and financial reconciliation.
Experience with taxation data and compliance reporting (e.g., Single Touch Payroll, BAS, PAYG).
Hands-on experience with payroll data and understanding of regulatory requirements.
Proficiency in SQL and programming languages such as Python, R, or Scala.
Experience with AWS data services including Glue and EventBridge.
Experience with data platforms like Snowflake or Databricks.
Understanding of secure data handling protocols and privacy standards.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

Reporting to the Manager ICT – Operations & Services, the ICT Systems Engineer will design, support and optimise MCM’s infrastructure environment across Azure cloud services, enterprise networking, storage platforms and backup technologies.

This role requires strong experience across Microsoft Azure, Office 365, networking infrastructure and automation, along with the ability to troubleshoot and manage enterprise platforms in a structured IT service delivery environment.

About you

We are looking for someone who can:

Design, deploy and support Microsoft Azure infrastructure and services

Manage and maintain Microsoft 365 services including Exchange Online, Entra ID and enterprise applications

Configure and manage Azure ExpressRoute connectivity

Implement and maintain Conditional Access policies and identity security controls

Support hybrid identity environments including Azure AD Connect and hybrid join

Manage and support enterprise networking infrastructure including HPE networking platforms

Support and maintain SD-WAN environments including Aruba Central

Implement and manage network access control using Aruba ClearPass

Manage and maintain enterprise certificate services and PKI infrastructure

Maintain and support SAN and NAS storage infrastructure

Manage Veeam backup and recovery environments

Develop PowerShell scripting and automation to improve operational efficiency

Manage Group Policy and endpoint configuration standards

Participate in infrastructure upgrades, cloud migrations and ICT projects

Provide Level 3 technical support and incident resolution

Maintain accurate infrastructure documentation and system standards

Benefits of working with MCM include:

Diverse and inclusive organisation

Attractive Salary Packaging benefits

Supportive learning and development environment

Culture of continuous improvement

Essential Requirements:

Minimum 6+ years’ experience as a Senior Systems Engineer

Experience working in a Managed Services Provider (MSP) or large enterprise environment

Strong experience with Microsoft Azure and Microsoft 365

Proven experience managing Azure ExpressRoute connectivity

Strong enterprise networking capability

Cisco Certified Network Professional (CCNP) certification or equivalent experience

Strong experience implementing network authentication and access control using Aruba ClearPass

Strong experience with SD-WAN technologies

Experience managing enterprise certificate services / PKI infrastructure

Experience managing SAN and NAS storage platforms

Proven experience administering Veeam backup and recovery

Strong PowerShell scripting and automation skills

Advanced experience with Group Policy management

Experience implementing Conditional Access and identity security policies

Experience supporting hybrid on-premises and Azure environments

Excellent troubleshooting, documentation and problem-solving skills

Desirable Skills

MCM is an inclusive and equal opportunity employer with a strong commitment to safety. MCM embraces differences because we know that diversity and inclusion help us to attract, recruit, engage and retain talented people.

Data Agility is a specialist data, analytics and AI firm delivering data governance, integration, migration, reporting and platform solutions across health, government and enterprise sectors.

This role works across both our Managed Services team and our Product team, supporting enterprise Microsoft data platforms and ensuring performance, stability and continuous improvement across client and product environments.

About the Role

This is a technical, client-facing role focused on operational support, optimisation and continuous improvement of Microsoft data platforms. You will work across production environments, ensuring high availability, performance, security and data integrity.

You will collaborate closely with internal teams and clients to diagnose issues, enhance ETL processes, optimise database performance and support reporting environments including Power BI.

Key Responsibilities

Provide day-to-day administration and operational support for enterprise Microsoft data and analytical platforms and Microsoft SQL database environments, ensuring high availability and performance

Work with our clients in the support and maintenance of their data platform and Power BI reporting environment including data lakes and data warehouses

Analyse, design, develop, tune and optimise ETL processes for new and existing data sources

Identify, analyse and resolve ETL processing issues and data quality problems

Analyse, design and develop requested changes such as new data sources, alterations to business rules and reports

Perform ad hoc queries, analysis and reporting, as and when required

Troubleshoot and fix bugs, working closely with our clients to ensure issues have been properly diagnosed, estimated and resolved

Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives, including documentation, automation and operational best practices

Requirements

Strong knowledge of end-to-end data warehouse environments across Microsoft Azure and Fabric

Strong knowledge and experience in ETL design and development

Strong knowledge of SQL querying including expertise in writing, optimising and debugging

Strong knowledge of SQL Server administration with a deep understanding of database design, indexing, normalisation and replication

Proven experience in performance tuning, query optimisation and troubleshooting database issues

Solid understanding of data security, access controls and compliance standards

Familiarity with automation, scheduling, deployment and monitoring tools (e.g. Data Factory, DevOps, SQL Server Agent) and version control (e.g. Git/Bitbucket)

Excellent problem-solving, attention to detail, and ability to document technical processes clearly

Effective communication and collaboration skills, working seamlessly with business and technical teams

Why Join Data Agility

Work across both client-managed environments and our Code Focus health product platform

Contribute to meaningful work supporting government, community organisations, hospitals and health services, where data quality and platform performance directly influence funding accuracy, reporting integrity and operational decision-making

Opportunity to contribute to platform optimisation and operational best practice

Great team culture with experienced, supportive colleagues who collaborate closely

Hybrid working environment offering flexibility and work life balance

How you'll make an impact

We're looking for a Data Engineer to join our Data Office team. In this role, you'll design, build, and maintain automated data pipelines that extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from multiple sources into our data warehouse. Your work will ensure data is clean, consistent, and accessible for analysis, enabling smarter decisions across the business.

You'll collaborate with data scientists, analysts, and business intelligence teams to deliver high-quality solutions from a prioritised backlog of incidents and demands. If you're proactive, detail-oriented, and passionate about data quality, this is the role for you.

Your usual day in the office will include:


Design and implement data pipelines from conceptual to physical stages.
Code, test, and maintain ETL processes in line with agreed designs.
Monitor and resolve production pipeline issues promptly.
Assist with release management and deployment processes.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver solutions that meet SLAs.

Your attributes

Degree in Computer Science, IT, or related field.
2+ years' experience in a similar role.
Proficiency in Spark SQL, PySpark, Python, SQL Server, SSIS, SSRS, Databricks, Azure Data factory (ADF), Power BI.
Experience with Jira, Confluence, and ServiceNow.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work under pressure.
Excellent time management, attention to detail, and collaboration skills.


Please note this role will require rostered shift work (7am - 6pm)

Our client AGL is seeking an experienced Senior Infrastructure / Systems Engineer to join a high-performing Operational Technology (OT) team supporting critical energy assets across Australia.This role plays a key part in integrating new and emerging technologies—including new datacentres, energy assets, network and server platforms, and storage systems—into existing OT environments. You’ll ensure seamless integration into BAU operations, strengthen OT infrastructure, and help uplift engineering capability across the team. This is a hands-on senior technical role suitable for someone who enjoys solving complex integration challenges, working across wide-ranging technologies, and blending new tech with older, legacy systems. This is a 12 month contract role based in Melbourne CBD in a hybrid working environment.
 
What You’ll Be Doing

Designing and integrating infrastructure solutions across networks, servers, firewalls, storage, and virtualisation platforms.
Supporting major OT initiatives including new energy assets, data centre upgrades, and broader infrastructure uplift.
Engaging with BAU teams to understand capability gaps, provide support, and identify training needs.
Working with both new and legacy technology to ensure smooth end-to-end system integration.
Applying strong infrastructure design principles—physical and logical—including power, cooling, cabling, rack layouts, and power diagrams.
Contributing to secure-by-design architecture in partnership with cybersecurity teams.
Producing clear engineering documentation for implementation and ongoing support.
Participating in project planning, vendor engagement, and technical reviews.

 
What You’ll Bring

Strong experience across network and operating system environments (Windows & Linux).
Hands-on knowledge of physical infrastructure: environmental considerations, power requirements, cooling, rack layouts, physical space planning.
Demonstrated experience integrating technologies using APIs, interface design, system architecture and multi-component integrations.
Solid experience with Palo Alto and Cisco networking/firewall technologies.
Strong network fundamentals—CCNA or equivalent experience ideal.
Knowledge across a broad mix of infrastructure technologies such as:
o    Routing & switching (VLANs, OSPF, BGP, spanning tree)
o    Firewalls (Next Gen)
o    Virtualisation (VMware, Hyper-V)
o    Storage (SAN – fibre channel or iSCSI)
o    Backup & recovery principles (RPO/RTO)
o    Monitoring & observability tools (SolarWinds, PRTG, Zabbix; Splunk/Elastic beneficial)
o    Cloud (Azure, ExpressRoute)
o    Automation tools (Ansible, Panorama, System Center)

Ability to mentor team members and support capability uplift.
Strong communication skills with the ability to work across technical and non-technical stakeholders.

 
Nice to Have
•    Exposure to OT environments:
SCADA systems, historians, industrial control systems, engineering tools.
Experience integrating technology into critical infrastructure environments.

At Chandler Macleod, we are committed to fostering a diverse workforce where everyone is welcome. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, women, neurodiverse, people living with a disability and the LGBTIQA+ community.

You can read more about our commitment to diversity and inclusion at https://www.chandlermacleod.com/diversity-and-inclusion.

The role

Are you passionate about data integrity and seamless system transitions? In this role, you’ll play a critical part in migrating data into Oracle Fusion ERP, ensuring accuracy, security, and performance every step of the way. You’ll work with cutting-edge tools like Oracle PL/SQL, Oracle Autonomous Database, and FBDI templates to extract, transform, and load data efficiently.

You’ll collaborate closely with business analysts and stakeholders, tackle complex data challenges, and help deliver a smooth migration that supports our business transformation. If you thrive on problem-solving and precision, this is your opportunity to make a real impact.

This is a 12-month contract, with potential to extend.

What you'll do
Design, develop, and test Oracle PL/SQL routines for ETL processes to move data efficiently and securely.
Analyse source and target data models, perform data mapping, and resolve data quality issues through cleansing and enrichment.
Validate and reconcile data between source and target systems.
Collaborate with business analysts, developers, and stakeholders to define requirements and resolve data challenges.
Support SIT and UAT phases, ensuring data integrity checks are completed.
Create technical documentation, data flow diagrams, and migration reports.
Participate in team meetings and workshops to drive successful outcomes.
What you'll bring
5+ years’ experience in data migration, particularly with Oracle databases and ERP environments.
Advanced Oracle PL/SQL skills and strong ETL process knowledge.
Familiarity with Oracle Finance and Procurement data sets would be highly advantageous
Experience with Oracle FBDI templates will help success in this role
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Why Join Us?

At Kinetic, we’re committed to improving the livelihoods of the communities we serve. We offer:

A collaborative and supportive team environment.
Opportunities to work on cutting-edge Oracle Cloud ERP projects.
A culture that prioritises safety, wellbeing, and continuous improvement.

Join Kinetic’s Digital Transformation Journey!
The role:

At Kinetic, we’re on a mission to improve the livelihoods of the communities in which we live and operate. We move people, enhance communities, and strive to be an employer of choice.

As part of our enterprise-wide digital and data transformation program, we’re seeking a Data Engineer to play a pivotal role in migrating data from legacy systems into modern platforms across Finance, HR, Payroll, Asset Management, and more.

What You’ll Do:
Perform data profiling and cleansing to identify and resolve data quality issues in collaboration with business SMEs and Data Governance Leads.
Support end-to-end data migration activities, including analysis, extraction, transformation, loading (ETL), validation, and post-load reconciliation.
Work with Data Governance Leads and SI Partners to define and maintain data mappings, transformation rules, and lineage.
Build data pipelines using APIs and databases with Azure services.
Apply CI/CD practices with tools like Azure DevOps.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data definitions and ownership structures.
Assist testing teams and SMEs in data validation during mock loads and cutovers.
Develop dashboards and reports to track migration progress and highlight data quality issues.
What You'll bring:
Tertiary qualification in Data Analytics, Information Systems, Business, Finance, or related field.
Strong data engineering/migration experience in an Azure environment, including at least one end-to-end migration project.
Proficiency in SQL, PySpark, Azure Data Factory (ADF).
Ability to create and maintain documentation (data mappings, dictionaries, validation rules).
High attention to detail, analytical mindset, and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively across technical and non-technical teams.
Why Join Us?
Be part of a transformational program shaping the future of Kinetic.
Work in a collaborative environment with passionate professionals.
Enjoy a culture that prioritises health, safety, and wellbeing.




Ready to make an impact? Apply now and help us deliver accurate, timely, and high-quality data migration that powers Kinetic’s future.

Job Title: Senior Manager, Data Science (Lead Data Scientist)
Ad Text:
We look forward to welcoming a Technical Leader who will lead projects with cutting-edge AI technologies and pave the way for innovative practices that benefit not only CommBank but the banking industry as a whole.

Do work that matters:

As part of Risk Management, you will participate in the development, testing, evaluation and deployment of models and AI solutions, to help Risk Management deliver on our Ambition, supporting the bank as we reimagine customer experiences and lead in AI.

See yourself in our team:

As a Lead Data Scientist, you will work with a team of data scientists and problem solvers using technologies and techniques from data science to drive decisions and risk outcomes.

Why CommBank?

CommBank is leading the world of banking by venturing into new-to-the-world innovations. We're on the cutting edge, embracing AI technologies to reshape how we operate and serve our customers. When you join CommBank, you’re joining a team that values expertise and innovation, and you’ll be empowered to lead projects that push the boundaries of what's possible.

Key Responsibilities:

• Demonstrate deep technical knowledge and specialist expertise to develop innovative solutions to complex technical problems, using advanced analytics techniques, including machine learning and natural language processing across large datasets.

• Generate and communicate insights aligned to business objectives and relevant to diverse stakeholders and clients, taking into consideration business roles and technical understanding of audiences.

• Lead and manage data scientists to achieve their best by creating a high-performance culture, providing guidance and coaching on appropriate modelling and statistical techniques.

• Provide strategic direction to the team and lead multiple projects to create scalable and reliable end-to-end solutions for our customers, prioritising with business stakeholders and balancing long-term objectives versus immediate delivery.

• Conduct extensive big data analysis, optimising data processing workflows, and deriving deeper insights from big data, while integrating big data solutions with cloud platforms.

• Design and implement more complex machine learning solutions, handling larger datasets, tuning models for better performance, and working on sophisticated problems.

• Apply practical experience in software engineering, participating in and contributing to software development projects, and ensuring best practices in coding standards, documentation, and code reviews.




We’re interested in hearing from people who have:

· Expertise in the design and construction of machine learning models, including feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter tuning, model evaluation, and deployment of predictive models into production environments.

· Familiarity with various aspects of Generative AI, including prompt engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), guardrail design, orchestration design, and tools such as LangChain and LangGraph.

· Worked on projects leveraging traditional AI, Generative AI, and Agentic AI to pave the path for how banking and broader industry functions in the future.

· Experience with version control and CI/CD pipelines such as GitHub.

· Familiarity with Docker for containerization and deployment of applications.

· Grounded understanding in AWS or Azure Cloud architecture and integration, demonstrating experience in deploying and managing cloud-based solutions.

· Tertiary Qualification in Statistics, Mathematics, Data Science or Computer Science.

Salary: fix-term salary or day rate option

Job Title: Mid Level Software Engineer - Ai Native Fintech
Ad Text:
Company Description


Lendi Group is a market-leading digital platform business that is focused on revolutionising the entire property journey. It operates Lendi - Australia’s original and #1 digital mortgage brand - and the iconic Aussie franchise.

Powered by an advanced AI proprietary platform, a national network of 1,300 brokers, 230 retail stores, a team of experienced buyer’s agents, integrated property search and conveyancing, Aussie is supporting customers at every step of their property journey to find, buy and own with more confidence than ever before. Lendi Group’s loan book is more than $100b and it was named Australia’s Aggregator of the Year for 2024.

To learn more about life at Lendi Group check out our blog: https://www.lendi.com.au/inspire/category/life-at-lendi/

Innovation at Our Core – We challenge the status quo and push boundaries to create better solutions, with a goal of becoming AI-native by June 2026.
Work with the Best – Collaborate with some of the brightest minds in fintech, financial services, and strategy.
Make an Impact – Contribute to meaningful projects that shape our business and the future of property finance.
Grow & Evolve – Develop your skills and advance your career in a fast-moving, purpose-driven environment.

About the role

Build the future of fintech — with AI at the core
Lendi Group is evolving into an AI-native fintech, embedding artificial intelligence into how we design, build, and operate our products. This isn’t about bolting on tools — it’s about empowering engineers to solve harder problems, move faster, and deliver smarter, more personalised experiences for millions of Australians.
We’re investing heavily in upskilling, experimentation, and modern engineering practices, giving our teams access to cutting-edge AI capabilities and the autonomy to shape how they’re used. If you’re curious, collaborative, and excited by what AI can unlock in real-world products — this is your moment.


We’re looking for a Mid Level Software Engineer to join our established team with high amount of BAU — You’ll work alongside passionate, experienced engineers tackling complex product and platform challenges, while helping define how AI is practically applied across our engineering lifecycle. You’ll have genuine influence — on architecture, engineering standards, delivery rhythm, and the evolution of an AI-enabled fintech platform — all in a team that values learning, ownership, and having fun while doing meaningful work.

Job Description


Own and deliver robust, scalable systems, influencing architecture and design decisions
Practise a true “you build it, you own it” mindset — from development to monitoring and support
Build clean, maintainable code with strong CI/CD practices and modern engineering standards

Work as a full-stack engineer, building backend services in Node.js (TypeScript) with React front-ends
Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, BAs, and Engineers in cross-functional agile squads
Partner with Product and UX to shape intuitive customer journeys and interaction patterns
Drive team rhythm across planning, delivery, deployment, and continuous improvement
Mentor and uplift engineers across the team and broader engineering group
Apply AI tools thoughtfully across the SDLC to improve productivity, quality, and outcomes

Qualifications


Core experience
5+ years of backend development experience
Strong proficiency in Node.js (TypeScript) and modern backend ecosystems
Strong API design skills: REST, schema-first design, GraphQL, webhooks, and auth (Auth0, JWT)
Deep understanding of microservices architectures and messaging patterns (event-driven, pub/sub, request-response)

Hands-on experience with Kafka, Docker, and Kubernetes
Strong AWS experience (IAM, Lambda, DynamoDB, ECS, SSM)
Experience with DevOps and Infrastructure-as-Code approaches

AI & modern engineering (new and evolving)

Strong understanding of AI tools and their practical use across the SDLC
Experience or exposure to AI engineering, including:
Building Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines
Prompt engineering for effective LLM use cases
Working with LLM frameworks such as LangChain, Semantic Kernel, Agno, or similar
Exposure to agentic AI (AI agents, task orchestration, automation workflows)
Willingness to work across a variety of AI tools as the ecosystem evolves

Nice to have (but not essential)

Experience with Kotlin (used in parts of our Partner Product space)
Exposure to MongoDB, including schema design, indexing, and change streams
Experience streaming or integrating data into platforms like Databricks
Analytics and observability tools such as Amplitude and Datadog
Experience with Fastify (or similar Node frameworks) and Zod schema validation
Knowledge of Auth0 + OpenFGA and multi-client authentication flows
Understanding of security and compliance in an AI-enabled environment

Why Lendi Group?
Build real products used by millions, not experiments in isolation

Be part of a genuine AI-native transformation, not just “AI theatre”
Learn, experiment, and lead with modern AI and cloud technologies
Work with smart, supportive engineers who value ownership and craft
Shape the future of fintech while continuing to grow your own skills

If you’re excited by AI-enabled engineering, ownership at scale, and building meaningful fintech products — we’d love to hear from you.

Additional Information


Lendi Group teams operate across Australia and the Philippines. Bringing together diverse expertise, innovative technology and a customer-first approach, our teams work seamlessly to simplify the property journey, helping Australians find, buy and own property with confidence.

We support our people in a variety of ways, but a few of the benefits that our people rave about include:

A vibrant, relaxed, yet professional culture.
Hybrid working arrangement designed to support work-life balance, while fostering meaningful connection and collaboration.
A holistic wellbeing programs offering 24/7 support, including medical, mental health, and financial wellbeing services to enable our workforce to thrive at home and work.
Generous paid Parental Leave: we celebrate our growing Lendi Group family with 18-26 weeks leave for primary carers and up to 4 weeks for secondary carers.
An additional week’s Loyalty Leave each year after reaching 3 years’ service.
Wellness initiatives with a strong focus on psychological safety.

We’re committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive community at Lendi Group. We believe that a team reflecting the world around us leads to greater innovation, stronger collaboration, and a more engaging workplace.

Our culture is guided by our 3 core values: We Are Stronger United; Act Like You Own It; and Keep Home Loans Human. Our values are part of our core DNA that helps Lendi Group to attract, engage and evolve the right talent and build best-in-class products.

This is an opportunity to shape the future of a fast-growing, purpose-driven company that’s transforming the homeownership journey.

Ready to contribute to Lendi Group’s next chapter? Apply now and be part of something big!

About the role:

The Security Engineer is a valued member of our engineering team. We value evidence-backed opinions and offer the opportunity to play an integral part in shaping engineering rhythm, culture, and processes across planning, architecture and design, coding, deployment, and support.
In an ever-changing environment, Lendi Group is leading the charge in developing industry-first technology designed to offer Australian homeowners transparency, simplicity, and convenience in their home loan experience.
The Security Engineer partners with engineering teams to evaluate planned changes, minimise security risk, and continuously improve our security posture. A key focus of the role is driving vulnerability and security findings down over time, improving security standards, and embedding secure-by-default practices across both traditional and AI-enabled systems.

Please note: this role will be a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract

Job Description


Working closely with the Cyber Security team to ensure security principles are consistently applied across the engineering environment.
Collaborating and coding with engineering teams to uplift secure development practices and reduce recurring security findings.
Designing, building, and maintaining secure cloud and microservice solutions.
Improving security posture through effective use of COTS products, platform controls, and network infrastructure.
Vulnerability and Threat Management — monitoring and assessing assets including cloud infrastructure, containers, packages, APIs, and requests, with a focus on remediation and measurable risk reduction.
Supporting the secure design and operation of AI-enabled systems, including LLM integrations, automation agents, and MCP-based architectures.
Implementing and reviewing AI security guardrails such as access controls, data protection, prompt-injection mitigation, and output validation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, operations, compliance, data, and AI platform teams.
Contributing as an individual contributor within a cross-functional DevOps / SecOps team.
Build or integrate services to improve security and compliance

Qualifications


The ideal candidate is passionate about promoting a security-first culture and has a strong, current understanding of security best practices, with particular focus on cloud security, secure design, incident handling, and operational resilience.
Experience securing microservices in cloud environments and working with configuration-as-code is important for this role. Experience securing AI-driven systems, including LLM-powered services and internal automation, is highly regarded.
Strong communication skills are essential. We value engineers who can clearly communicate risk, trade-offs, and remediation paths, and who approach collaboration with empathy for engineering challenges.

Key Skills

Strong understanding of networking, infrastructure, and applications from a DevOps perspective with a security focus.
Hands-on experience implementing and maintaining security controls across cloud environments (particularly AWS) and access management solutions (e.g. Auth0).
Experience with security logging, monitoring, and vulnerability assessment, with a demonstrated ability to drive findings down and improve baseline security standards.
Passion for secure coding practices and identifying common implementation and operational security risks.

Desirable Experience

AWS technologies such as CloudFormation, Terraform, IAM, WAF, Node.js and/or TypeScript.
Docker, containerised environments, serverless architectures, and Kubernetes.
Event-driven systems.
Securing AI platforms, including LLM integrations, MCP implementations, AI guardrails, and automation/build agents.
